content.full_text:
California 
Fair Political 
Practices Commission 
Rita" Walters 
Board of Education 
City of Los Angeles 
450 North Grand Avenue 
Los Angeles, CA 
90051 
Dear Ms. Walters: 
January 9, 1990 
RE: 
Your Request for Advice 
Our File No. A-89-666 
You have requested advice concerning the campaign disclosure 
provisions of the Political Reform Act (the "Act") .1/ 
FACTS 
You have been advised by counsel that recent court actions 
have affected FPPC regulations relating to the utilization of 
campaign funds possessed prior to the effective date of 
Proposition 73. 
You have established two separate bank accounts 
so that contributions received prior to January 1, 1989 may be 
distinguished from contributions received after this date. 
QUESTION 
What are the permissible uses of campaign funds received 
prior to January 1, 19897 
CONCLUSION 
Candidates and officeholders may use all funds received prior 
to January 1, 1989 to support their own candidacy. 
Therefore, the 
funds you raised prior to January 1, 1989, may be used to support 
your candidacy for any elective office. 
Enclosed is FPPC advice 
letter I-89-378 which provides the basis for this conclusion and 
an analysis. 
Effective January 1, 1990, certain restrictions on the use of 
campaign funds were added to the Act. 
(Chapter 1452, Stats. 
1989.) 
Previously, these provisions were contained in the 
1/ Government Code sections 81000-91015. 
All statutory references 
are to the Government Code unless otherwise indicated. 
Commission 
regulations appear at 2 California Code of Regulations section 
18000, et seq. 
All references to regulations are to Title 2, 
Division 6 of the California Code of Regulations. 
428 J Street, Suite 800 • P.O. Box 807 • Sacramento CA 95804,0807 • (916)322,5660 


Rita Walters 
Page 2 
California Elections Code. 
In general, the new provisions 
(Sections 85800-85807) require that expenditures of campaign funds 
be reasonably related to a political, legislative or governmental 
purpose. 
Enclosed is a fact sheet which explains the provisions. 
Finally, please note that, pursuant to Section 85304, a 
candidate may not transfer his or her campaign funds to another 
candidate or to a committee which will use the funds to support or 
oppose candidates for office. 
Please do not hesitate to contact me at 916/322-5662 if you 
need additional assistance on this question. 
Enclosure 
Sincerely, 
Kathryn E. Donovan 
General Counsel

California 
Fair Political 
Practices Commission 
November 17, 1989 
Rita Walters 
Board of Education city of 
Los angeles 
450 North Grand Avenue 
Los Angeles, CA 
90051 
Dear Ms. Walters: 
Re: 
Letter No. 89-666 
Your letter requesting advice under the Political Reform Act 
was received on November 20, 1989 by the Fair Political Practices 
Commission. 
If you have any questions about your advice request, 
you may contact me directly at (916) 322-5662. 
We try to answer all advice requests promptly. 
Therefore, 
unless your request poses particularly complex legal questions, or 
more information is needed, you should expect a response within 21 
working days if your request seeks formal written advice. 
If more 
information is needed, the person assigned to prepare a response 
to your request will contact you shortly to advise you as to the 
information needed. 
If your request is for informal assistance, 
we will answer it as quickly as we can. 
(See Commission 
Regulation 18329 (2 Cal. Code of Regs. Sec. 18329).) 
You also should be aware that your letter and our response 
are public records which may be disclosed to the public upon 
receipt of a proper request for disclosure. 
JP:plh 
Very truly yours, 
~n~ne J~Q\L~ Jd-
Technical Assistance 
Analysis Division 
, 
428 J Street, Suite 800 • P.O. Box 807 • Sacramento CA 95804..0807 • (916)322 .. 5660
